_comment: Config for sync-stats.py. Copy to sync-stats.config.json and edit. The live file is gitignored.

sources.label: Data Suite 2025 season totals
sources._field_mapping_help: Map canonical field names (left) to the exact CSV header text in your file (right). Canonical names known to downstream code: name (REQUIRED), team, pos, sleeperId, games, passAtt, passCmp, passYards, passTd, passInts, rushAtt, rushYards, rushTd, targets, rec, recYards, recTd, fumbles. Headers not present in the CSV are skipped with a warning.

_examples_to_pattern_after: Adding a second source (e.g. per-week stats appended into the same stats.json): duplicate the sources entry, point at the new CSV, set its field mapping.
Sources are merged in array order. Later sources OVERWRITE fields on conflicting keys (so put canonical season totals FIRST and any supplemental sources after).
When sleeperId is present in a row, the player is keyed by 'sid:<id>'. When absent, keyed by 'name:<normalized name>'. Downstream JS picks both up.
If your CSV uses different column names than the example above, just edit the right-hand side of the field mapping. The left-hand side names are fixed and must match the canonical field list.
